top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering analog explicitly self-tags as scaffolding: 'this verse is narrative scaffolding, not engineering doctrine,' and renders 'I will not fight' (na yotsya iti) as 'I am not going to lead this migration' followed by silence. The explicit naming of 'the silence is structurally significant — it is the precondition for the senior teacher's reply in the next verse' points to the Sanjaya-narrated end of Arjuna's case before Krishna replies.
Possible if read as Arjuna's collapse — but 1.46 is the chariot-drop, not the formal 'I will not fight' declaration.
Possible if read as the structural pivot — but 2.10 is Krishna's smile, not Arjuna's silence.
